cmes.note: Slow, wide CME to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink gap. The source for this CME is likely a diffuse, very slow dimming, spanning a filament channel approximately ~S26E58 to ~S08E25 (center S17E41), observed starting around 2026-04-16T21:30Z and lasting 12+ hours in SDO AIA 171/193/304 as the dimming, field line movement, and double ribbon flare, but not observing the filament itself actually leaving the disk. A bright inner core follows the initial front likely from this same source, measuring ~210 km/s at S16E52 with a half-width 27 degrees. Partially overlaps with following CME: 2026-04-17T07:53Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation, tracking the front direction with a bit of discrepancy on the width between the two coronagraphs (COR2A's view is used for the width).

cmes.note: Faint CME to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink gap. The source may be observed in GOES SUVI 171 over the NW limb starting around 2026-04-17T07:52Z, not observed in STEREO A EUVI despite the likely far-sided source.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: Slow, wide CME to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 and to the SSW in STEREO A COR2, not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink gap. The source is likely far-sided, observed as slow rising and erupting field line loops over the SE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting as early as approximately 2026-04-17T03:00Z lasting for several hours. Partially overlaps with prior CME: 2026-04-17T02:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: Very faint CME first seen to the SE in STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-04-17T10:23Z for a few frames. It is also seen to the S in GOES CCOR-1. This CME is covered by a real-time data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. There is not a good source candidate for this CME on the Earth-facing disk despite have a front-sided, triangulated measurement. A possibility is a very small eruption in the far south near the central meridian beginning around 2026-04-17T09:30Z best seen in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. There is a higher than normal degree of uncertainty in this measurement due to poor image quality.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-04-17T19:24Z. This CME is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and for a couple frames in GOES CCOR-1. It is covered by a real-time data in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is possibly a small eruption from an unnumbered region centered around N00E50 and is best seen in SDO AIA 304 starting around 2026-04-17T18:03Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-04-17T23:45Z. It is only seen in GOES CCOR-1 in real time due to data gaps in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. There is no good source candidate for this CME on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to single coronagraph view and lack of source candidate.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a filament eruption spanning S32E55 to S20E30 which begins to lift off around 2026-04-18T08:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. Opening field lines can be seen over the southeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193, GOES SUVI 171/195284, and STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery. Additionally, post eruptive dimming is seen in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 195, with post eruptive loops visible in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/193/284 imagery starting around 2026-04-18T10:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This faint outflow like CME is visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 due to the pilon and it is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a nightly data gap from 2026-04-19T19:09Z to 2026-04-19T02:53Z. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit using GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Similar parameters were achieved with a SOHO LASCO C2 plane-of-sky fit with less imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source is not clearly seen on-disk in available SDO AIA or STEREO A EUV imagery, but opening field lines may be seen starting around 2026-04-19T15:13Z in SDO AIA 171 imagery in ISWA.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement based on SOHO LASCO C2/C3 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to E in STEREO A COR2 imagery. Not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 currently due to data gaps. Eruption near N05E35 located just south of a coronal hole in the NE starting around 2026-04-19T14:42Z as seen in SDO/AIA 193, 171, and 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on potential source location and best fit using STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint narrow jet-like outflow CME to the E/NE in SOHO LASCO C2. Not clearly seen in available SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, or STEREO A COR2 imagery. The possible source is a potential emerging unnumbered Active Region beyond the Eastern limb which appears to be releasing numerous outflows as it rotates further into view in SDO/AIA 193 and 171.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 frames. Only 4 usable frames before a separate jet-like outflow CME overtakes this feature.

cmes.note: Faint narrow jet-like outflow CME to the E/NE in SOHO LASCO C2 with a very diffuse front. Not clearly seen in available SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, or STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME overtakes the CME first seen to the E/NE in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-04-20T01:48Z. The potential source is a possible emerging unnumbered Active Region beyond the Eastern limb which appears to be releasing numerous outflows as it rotates further into view in SDO/AIA 193 and 171.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit of the feature in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. Very diffuse front not easily trackable, only 4 frames used for this measurement.

cmes.note: Faint narrow jet-like outflow CME to the E/NE in SOHO LASCO C2. Not clearly seen in available SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, or STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source is a possible emerging unnumbered Active Region beyond the Eastern limb which appears to be releasing numerous outflows as it rotates further into view in SDO/AIA 193 and 171.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters using best fit of feature in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. Front is very diffuse and disappears/dissolves after a few frames. Only 3 frames used in this analysis.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-04-21T00:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is field line opening seen originating from beyond the SE limb in SDO AIA 193 beginning at approx. 2026-04-20T21:51Z. This CME overlaps with later CME: 2026-04-21T09:53Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Bright CME first seen to the N by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-04-21T02:00Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 in later frames. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen centered at approx. N30E10 beginning at approx. 2026-04-21T01:32Z as seen in SDO AIA/GOES SUVI 304. A wide region of dimming can be seen following this eruption in SDO AIA 193, along with field line opening visible in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-04-21T02:12Z, as well as by STEREO A COR2, GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 in later frames. Th source of this event is a filament eruption seen originating from beyond the SW limb in SDO AIA/GOES SUVI 304, with field line opening seen in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 284. This filament begins to liftoff at approx. 2026-04-21T01:37Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Diffuse CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-04-21T06:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is field line opening seen along the NE limb beginning at approx. 2026-04-21T04:25Z as seen in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event is partially obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-04-21T09:53Z. This CME is obscured by earlier CME: 2026-04-21T00:00Z in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOEA CCOR-1 and is difficult to identify. The source of this event is an eruption from just along/beyond the SE limb beginning at approx. 2026-04-21T08:57Z as seen in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI284. Post eruptive arcades are also visible in SDO AIA 131 and 211.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A only measurement based on source location and best fit, as CME is difficult to distinguish from earlier CME: 2026-04-21T00:00Z in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-04-21T10:23Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink data gap which began at 2026-04-21T10:24Z. The source of this CME is likely continued dimming and field line movement following an earlier filament eruption associated with CME: 2026-04-21T02:00Z, seen located at approx. N50E20 as seen in SDO AIA 193

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the N in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. No direct source for this CME is observed in available imagery, but it could be outflow from the large, wide CME: 2026-04-21T02:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only plane-of-sky. The front is a bit fragmented and may be multiple tiny outflow-like fronts traveling together at the same speed, analyzed in the same fit.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This event is not observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely far-sided and may be related to an eruption observed over the eastern limb in SDO AIA 304 imagery starting around 2026-04-22T04:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-2 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no clear source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: Minor CME seen to the northwest in GOES/CCOR-1 and SOHO/C2 and SOHO/C3 coronagraphs and due north in STEREO A/COR2 in association with a minor eruption in AR 4419 (~W52) starting around 2026-04-23T22:45Z. The eruption is seen best in GOES SUVI 284 as a brightening and the expulsion of plasma to the northeast.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using only GOES and SOHO imagery as there was a STEREO A data gap at the time of analysis.

cmes.note: Jet CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 but not in STEREO A due to a data gap; the source is a pinpoint eruption just near or beyond the eastern limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky leading edge measurement as the longitude is not certain.

cmes.note: CME seen to the northwest in GOES/CCOR-1 and SOHO/C2 and SOHO/C3 coronagraphs and due north in STEREO A/COR2 in association with an M1.6-class solar flare in AR 4419 (~W52) starting around 2026-04-23T04:30Z. The eruption is seen best in GOES SUVI 284 as a brightening and the expulsion of plasma to the northeast. Also seen in STEREO A EUV with an eruptive signature around center-disk. Deflected to the north based on the presentation/directionality in STEREO A coronagraphs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO, GOES, and STEREO A coronagraphs to ascertain the longitude; there is clear deflection to the north based on the measurement.

cmes.note: CME whose bulk is seen to the northeast with wider shock seen to due east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs in association with a long duration M1.2 class solar flare from AR4420 near N16E40. The EUV signature is characterized by a circular EUV wave and dimming signature during the eruption seen best in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge of bulk of the left-most edge (in the ecliptic plane) of the CME bulk which is fairly asymmetric; the portion out of the ecliptic generally moves slower relative to the portion in the ecliptic.
Measurement of the more symmetric shock front which much better fitting using triangulation relative to the asymmetric bulk.

cmes.note: CME seen to the northwest in GOES/CCOR-1 and SOHO/C2 and SOHO/C3 coronagraphs and due north in STEREO A/COR2 in association with an M4.3-class solar flare in AR 4419 (~W60) starting around 2026-04-23T08:45Z. The eruption is seen best in GOES SUVI 284 as a brightening and the expulsion of plasma to the northeast. Also seen in STEREO A EUV with an eruptive signature with significant dimming around center-disk. Deflected to the north based on the presentation/directionality in STEREO A coronagraphs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using triangulation between al three coronagraphs to ascertain the longitude.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be far-sided as there are no sources on the Earth-facing disk that align with the timing and direction of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 90 degrees due to the uncertain source location.

cmes.note: This relatively narrow CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery and is better seen in COR2A than in other coronagraphs. The source may be related to a dimming region centered near N10E30 observed starting around 2026-04-23T11:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and to the north in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption and M1.7 flare from Active Region 14419 (N17W66) starting around 2026-04-23T14:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. An EUV wave is seen moving north and east of the eruption site, with an opening of field lines observed over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME seen towards the N in STEREO A COR2 and to the NW in GOES CCOR-1. Its source its likely a minor eruption from AR 14419 (N18W65) seen starting around 2026-04-23T16:00Z as a relatively slowly developing, northward-deflecting ejecta in SDO AIA 304/131 and GOES SUVI 304, and as minor post-eruptive arcades in SDO AIA 193 and STEREO A EUV 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate preliminary analysis based on the fit with two available coronagraphs (GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2) in the absence of SOHO LASCO data due to scheduled temporary data gap. There seems to be NW deflection from the source (AR 14419 at N18W65).

cmes.note: CME seen to the NNE in STEREO A COR2 (for a few timestamps before the evening data gap in STEREO A) and to the NW in GOES CCOR-1. Its source is the M4.9-class flare from Active Region 14419 (N15W67) and the associated prominence eruption seen as northward deflecting, rotating ejecta in SDO AIA 304/171/195, GOES SUVI 284/304 and STEREO A 304 after 2026-04-23T17:03Z and as post-eruptive arcades in SDO AIA 193 and STEREO A 195. There is also an EUV wave seen in AIA 171/195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate preliminary analysis with GOES CCOR-1 and early STEREO A COR2 imagery (before STEREO A night time daily data gap). There seems to be a NW deflection from the source location of Active Region 14419 (N15W67).
